norm_title: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
sector: constitutional; human rights; public administration; health
subsector: public health; federalism; administrative legality; treaties; legislative process; oversight
issuing_authority: Cámara de Diputados del H. Congreso de la Unión / Secretaría de Servicios Parlamentarios
legal_function: establishes constitutional principles; distributes competences; creates oversight mechanisms; incorporates or governs international commitments
official_source: Converted from PDF (CPEUM-20.pdf) via Cámara de Diputados
main_internal_units: Titles, Chapters, Sections, Articles, Transitory Articles
relevance_for_ihr: High — foundational: Art. 1 (human rights and pro persona principle), Art. 4 (right to health), Art. 73 frac. XVI (federal health powers), Consejo de Salubridad General, treaty reception
relevance_for_pandemic_agreement: High — constitutional basis for treaty obligations, emergency powers and health governance framework
relevance_for_legal_anchoring: High — supreme normative anchor for all legal analysis in NormTrace-IHR
notes: Apex norm of the Mexican legal system. Post-2011 Art. 1 reform created a block of constitutionality (bloque de constitucionalidad) that may elevate international human rights treaties to constitutional rank. SCJN jurisprudence on hierarchy between CPEUM and international human rights treaties is complex. Requires manual review for nuanced hierarchy analysis. Original publication date (1917-02-05) not in metadata.

relevance_for_legal_anchoring: Moderate — procedural basis for health administrative acts and regulatory instruments
notes: General administrative procedure statute applicable to federal public administration. Has supplementary application to sector-specific laws including health sector. Original publication date not in metadata.

norm_title: Ley Orgánica de la Administración Pública Federal
official_source: Converted from PDF (D_LOAPF-10.pdf) via Cámara de Diputados
relevance_for_ihr: High — defines Secretaría de Salud institutional mandate, competences of intersecretarial bodies; key for identifying IHR National Focal Point and competent authorities
relevance_for_pandemic_agreement: High — institutional architecture for pandemic response coordination and inter-ministerial governance
relevance_for_legal_anchoring: High — competence anchor for health sector institutional actors; needed to verify delegation chains
notes: Organic law under Art. 90 CPEUM. Note: in Mexico, leyes orgánicas do not have special supralegislative rank; they operate at federal law level. Key for identifying Secretarías with health mandates, interministerial coordination and Consejo de Salubridad General administrative placement. Original publication date not in metadata.

norm_title: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
subsector: public administration; health governance; epidemiological surveillance; sanitary control; international health relations
issuing_authority: Presidencia de la República — Presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo; refrendado por David Kershenobich Stalnikowitz, Secretario de Salud
legal_function: creates institutional mandates; distributes competences; regulates health governance; regulates emergency powers
relevance_for_ihr: High — Art. 35 frac. XIX designates DGE as IHR national focal point liaison (enlace para el RSI); defines surveillance, laboratory, emergency and international coordination competences
relevance_for_pandemic_agreement: High — defines institutional architecture for health emergency response, coordination between units, COFEPRIS role, SNSP deployment
relevance_for_legal_anchoring: High — active source for mapping administrative competences, actor attributions and IHR anchoring at reglamentary level
notes: Complete 2025 version replacing the 2004 Reglamento Interior and all partial reforms. Abrogates RI DOF 19-01-2004. Active source for NormTrace-IHR. Key articles: Art. 2 (administrative structure); Art. 7 (Titular competences); Art. 33 (DG Relaciones Internacionales); Art. 35 (DGE — RSI focal point); Art. 43 (Servicio Nacional de Salud Pública); Art. 52 (CENAPRECE). COFEPRIS listed as deconcentrated body (Art. 2 C frac. II). Conversion from PDF; legal validation recommended before citing provisions.
